From the IPP, the most likely continuation is north/northwest along the black hiking trail rather than backtracking or leaving the trail immediately. As the hiker continued north, the terrain would funnel movement into the narrow trail/stream corridor. The contours tighten on both sides, so leaving the trail laterally would become less attractive, while continuing along the path would seem like the easiest and most reasonable option. The first high-priority search point is therefore where that northbound trail reaches the larger east-west stream/trail bend area, just before/at the point where route choice becomes less obvious. A tired or disoriented hiker could stop there, follow the stream, or mistakenly continue around the bend.
